subject: Vectrix dashboard outage last night — what happened?

messages.subject: Vectrix dashboard outage last night — what happened?
messages.body_text:
Hi Priya,

Our team was working late last night (Dec 23) and the Vectrix dashboard went down around 10:30pm PT for roughly 90 minutes. More concerning: no alerts came through to our Slack integration — our on-call engineer had no idea there was a problem until a customer reported it.

This is the first outage we've experienced since signing up in October. Can someone walk me through what happened and what the plan is going forward?

— Kevin Park
Head of Engineering, Brainloop
kevin.park@brainloop.io

messages.subject: Out of Office: Vectrix dashboard outage last night — what happened?
messages.body_text:
Hi,

I'm out of the office December 23 through January 2 with limited email access.

For urgent product or infrastructure issues, please contact Marcus Webb at marcus@vectrix.io.

I'll respond to your message when I return January 3.

Best,
Priya Chandran
CEO, Vectrix Labs

messages.subject: Following up on last night's Vectrix outage
messages.body_text:
Hi Marcus,

Reaching out directly — Priya is out. Can you fill me in on what happened last night with the Vectrix dashboard? I need to be able to explain it to our VP.

Main questions:
1. What caused the outage?
2. Why didn't our Slack alerts fire?
3. What changes are coming?

— Kevin Park
Head of Engineering, Brainloop
kevin.park@brainloop.io

messages.subject: Re: Following up on last night's Vectrix outage
messages.body_text:
Hi Kevin,

Thank you for reaching out, and I'm sorry for the disruption. Here's the full picture:

Root cause: A rate limiter bug introduced in our 10:15pm deploy caused it to reject all inbound API requests. Jordan caught it at 11:45pm and rolled back the deploy. Service fully restored at 11:58pm — approximately 90 minutes of downtime.

Why your Slack alerts didn't fire: Our alert evaluation runs through the same API layer that was rate-limited. When the limiter blocked requests, alert evaluation stopped too. This is a design flaw — alert evaluation should be isolated from API traffic. We are fixing it and shipping the fix in our next release (target: Jan 6).

What we're doing:
1. Decouple alert evaluation from the API layer (ships Jan 6)
2. Add pre-deploy smoke tests for rate limiter config changes
3. Formalize on-call rotation — Jordan was the sole engineer on-call Dec 23. That won't happen again.

Formal post-mortem will be in your customer portal by December 27.

Again, I'm sorry. The alert failure in particular is unacceptable — that's the thing we exist for.

— Marcus Webb
CTO, Vectrix Labs
marcus@vectrix.io
